title: podman 5.0.1 crashing with "fatal error: concurrent map writes"
body:
### Issue Description

Since upgrading to podman 5 for our GitLab runner we've been experiencing podman crashing with a "fatal error: concurrent map writes" error occasionally while the system is under load.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

We don't have any simple reproducing case.  It happens regularly while the machine is running 100 gitlab CI jobs at once.


### Describe the results you received

Podman process crashes with "fatal error: concurrent map writes".

Full backtrace [attached](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/14934073/podman-crash.log).

### Describe the results you expected

Podman not crashing.

comments.body:
The stack trace points to https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/17f36a369035b4d5c27f416af9cb961ede807eed/pkg/api/handlers/compat/networks.go#L124

And that definitely writes the map (i.e. deletes a item), the code is broken as it should clone the map first otherwise it will change options in the backend which is not good. I guess I thought `options := network.Options` would clone the map but that is not the case. Easy enough to fix, I will open a PR.

This code was added in https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/52656887e1cd3476a18765aa81a7804fceb861b4 for v4.3 so I am surprised that you just now notice this in 5.0, likely some other change that triggered it.

Hope its ok to chime in.
It seems we're affected by this as well, but with podman 4.9.3 / 4.9.4, a gitlab-runner instance, too.
Trace from line 153 in this version.
Is there any chance to have the fix merged to 4.9 and released?

Not sure if it applies cleanly but if someone wants to do to the work to backport and tests then maybe.
We only support the latest release, 4.9 might get further security releases so it could get picked up in the next security release but no promises.

Any reason you cannot move to 5.0?

Thanks!

I was unaware of podman's release strategy regarding older branches, thus the 4.9 release question.

I have no idea of go, but I can of course try to create a blind PR and see if it satisfies builds. If not, I'm probably out of the game.  We'll also try to build locally to test the fix with 4.9.

We just recently upgraded to ubuntu 24.04 hosts which packages podman 4.9.3 as "outer" podman, and combine with 4.9.4 as inner podman. There is no hard blocker to 5.0 (we didn't try yet), but if we can avoid putting additional time into building this major upgrade ourselfs, we'd try to keep 4.9 for now.
Looks like it can be backported cleanly https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/22835. If this is merged we should get another 4.9 release soon with a security fix as well
